body.title: [triage] The uniform assertion envelope: {claim, evidence, provenance_class, falsifier, horizon} on every artifact in the store — the store records WHAT was concluded but not HOW ANYTHING KNOWS IT
body.intention: I intend that every assertion in the memory store carries what produced it and what would invalidate it, so that a cached answer can be safely reused — because a store that records conclusions without their evidence is a confabulation store with good uptime.
body.description:
**RATIFIED by Akien 2026-07-13 ('i agree with fable'). This is the ONE structural move that makes four separate arguments unnecessary to have again.**

THE THESIS OF THIS PROJECT is that answered questions become STRUCTURE — the expensive resolver is needed only for the genuinely novel. But **a cached answer is only safely reusable if it carries WHAT PRODUCED IT and WHAT WOULD INVALIDATE IT.** Otherwise the cache is, in Fable's words, **'a confabulation store with good uptime'** — and we rebuild today's incident at every layer: intentions that DESCRIBE rather than BIND; records that LIE IN THE SHAPE OF A VIRTUE; validations that PARK FOREVER; levers that were never PAID FOR.

**THE ENVELOPE (uniform, every category):**
  `claim` — the assertion itself.
  `evidence` — what conditioned it. NAMED, not implied.
  `provenance_class` — declared | extracted-upstream | extracted-from-artifact | measured | inherited. **MECHANICALLY SET AT THE WRITE PATH, NEVER HAND-TYPED** (see falsifier).
  `falsifier` — what would make this false. An EVENT, not a condition.
  `horizon` — when we could know.

**HOW EACH OF TODAY'S FOUR ARGUMENTS IS A SHADOW OF THIS ONE:**
  1. **The intention circularity fight** was confused because AUTHORSHIP was standing in for EVIDENCE. Label an intention with what conditioned it and the machine-vs-human question EVAPORATES — a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical, so authority never lived in the typing. (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property.)
  2. **The intent-extractor's 2,435 uninterpretable records** exist because the CAUSE was collapsed out of the stored value. Provenance ON THE RECORD ('this value came from an except-block') makes the virtue-camouflage STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE. (R-feedback-is-unconditional.)
  3. **PENDING-VALIDATION** is, properly seen, just **provenance with a future tense** — a claim plus a DATED EVIDENCE PLAN. (T-intentions-carry-validations.)
  4. **'Naming a lever is a debt, not a credit'** is a demand that a lever claim carry OBSERVED RECURRENCES as evidence, not FORECASTS. (rules/lever-definition.)

**WHAT THIS IS.** CP1 ('I don't know') and CP3 ('there's always a why') applied to the MEMORY instead of to the TICKETS. The gate we already enforce on a ticket close — *point at the proof or name what you lack* — enforced on every assertion we store.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py (THE chokepoint — the class must be set HERE, from the write path, not from the author's keyboard); devlab/runtime/memory/{intentions,decisions,rules,proofs,notes,questions}/; validity_sweep.py (an assertion with no falsifier is a flaggable condition); consequence_aging.py (fires the horizon); skills/intend + /sorted (must ASK for evidence and falsifier at authoring time).

**Design rules:** homogeneity-over-special-case (ONE envelope, every category — not five bespoke schemas); resist-artifact-proliferation (extend memory_emit, do not birth a parallel store); CP1; CP3.

**Scope boundary:** IN — the envelope schema; enforcement at the memory_emit chokepoint; the provenance_class taxonomy and its MECHANICAL derivation; backfill strategy for existing entries (flag, never fabricate — CP2). OUT — retro-fitting provenance onto the 2,435 poisoned prediction records (they are unrecoverable IN PRINCIPLE; annotate as error, never delete — that is T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal).

**THE HARD PART, STATED HONESTLY (CP1):** backfilling provenance onto EXISTING entries is EXACTLY the circularity trap in a new costume — inferring 'what evidence conditioned this' FROM the entry itself. Existing entries get `provenance_class: unknown` and are FLAGGED. **They do not get a guess.** An assertion whose provenance we invented is worse than one that admits it has none.

**Proof obligation.** The hollow build adds five keys to every JSON file, runs a presence-check, and declares done. What a hollow build could NOT pass: (1) an entry emitted through memory_emit whose `provenance_class` was HAND-TYPED as `declared` when the write path was machine-driven is REJECTED — the class is derived from the CALLER, not the payload; (2) an entry with a falsifier phrased as a CONDITION rather than an EVENT is flagged; (3) a backfilled legacy entry carries `unknown` and is NOT silently upgraded.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. The envelope is in the schema and ENFORCED at the memory_emit chokepoint for every category.
  2. provenance_class is set MECHANICALLY from the write path; a hand-typed class is rejected.
  3. Legacy entries carry `unknown` + a flag; zero are fabricated.
  4. validity_sweep flags assertions with no falsifier.
  5. The red run (N assertions / 0 with provenance) is recorded before the green.

**Falsifier for this ticket's own intention:** if, after the envelope ships, an assertion is found carrying a provenance_class it did not earn, the class is DECORATIVE and this discipline has failed exactly as authorship did.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
